rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/23. (No earlier availability.) 1. Notice must include the instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] 2. A proposed order is lodged, not FILED, so it should not be listed on the Notice of Hearing. 3. The amended petition caption indicates that the personal representative is seeking statutory compensation, which he waived, but does not disclose attorney fees requested. [CRC 7.102.] 4. The decedent's date of death is not set forth in the pleading. 5. There are still references to an accounting in paragraph 5 of the amended petition and in the prayer. 6. There are still numerous erroneous references to the "administrator", when the personal representative is an EXECUTOR... but "administration of the estate" in paragraph 4 was changed for some reason to "executor of the estate", which makes no sense. 7. If all cash was invested in interest-bearing accounts, how is cash on hand exactly the same as on the date of death, 7/3/16? 8. Why were fiduciary income tax returns filed for 2014 and 2015? The decedent died in 2016, and the executor was not appointed until 2021. 9. Was the decedent's final personal income tax return filed and were taxes, if any, paid? 10. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN CONFINED in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. 11. Paragraphs 4, 5, and 6 of the prayer refer to erroneous paragraphs in the report. In any event, the specific asset(s) to be distributed to the specific beneficiary/ies should be in the prayer, not just in a reference to the report. 12. Cash on hand is reportedly $205,146.01 and Executor proposes to distribute that full amount to the trustees of the devisee trust (who were not identified in Exhibit B). Will attorney fees be paid from another source? 13. Why did the successor trustee sign this amended petition? She has no authority to allege anything in this estate. 14. A "Supplement to Clear Probate Notes" is unnecessary; an amended petition itself should include all of the correct or corrected allegations and disclosures. The courtesy copies of the second amended petition and proof of service of notice of the second amended petition must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/29/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and DIVIDED FROM THE REST OF THE DOCUMENT PAGE BY A PRINTED LINE. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LINDA L. TERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration/Terminating Of Anti-Human, Violent, Based On Fraud Date 4-Years Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is "Motion for Reconsideration/Terminating of Anti-Human, Violent, Based on Fraud Data 4-Years Restraining Order; With Direct Open Address to Presiding Judge" filed by Restrained Party Alexander Koustoph. The Motion is DENIED, for the following reasons. Insofar as the document before the court is intended to be a Request to Modify/Terminate Elder or Dependent Adult Abuse Restraining Order, it is denied as procedurally defective. (See Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657.03(f); Mandatory Judicial Counsel Form EA-600). Insofar as the document before the court is intended to be a motion for reconsideration of either the April 27, 2022 Elder or Dependent Adult Abuse Restraining Order After Hearing or the August 18, 2022 Order denying Petition for Extra Hearing with the Judge brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, it is denied as procedurally defective. It is untimely, having been filed more than 10 days after Notice of Entry of that order (see Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a)). There is no also proof of service of this document filed with the court (see Code of Civil Procedure section 1005, 1005.5, 1014). Insofar as the document before the court is intended to be a motion for reconsideration of either the April 27, 2022 Elder or Dependent Adult Abuse Restraining Order After Hearing or the August 18, 2022 Order denying Petition for Extra Hearing brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, it is also denied on substantive grounds. The party moving for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure 1008 must meet the jurisdictional burden of filing the required affidavit with the required elements, showing both (1) "new or different" facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the order, and (2) a sufficient explanation for why the "new or different" information was not offered before the order issued. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a); Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc. (1998) 62 Cal. App. 4th 658, 670.) Koustoph has shown no "new or different" facts, law, or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the court's restraining order. Counsel for Conservator David Keeler shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY JANE HALLIDAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Exhibits D and E just show dates of birth and death. How did the administrator confirm that Archibald Crosby Halliday and Christopher W. Halliday were not survived by issue? (2) Exhibit H shows John (Jack) Mathes's date of death as 6/15/2000, not 1951, and does not show who his parents were. (3) Exhibit I shows the father of Aaron Mathes as Jack Eugene Mathes. There is no document showing that John (Jack) Mathes was aka Jack Eugene Mathes and married to Carole Margaret Baldwin (if they were married). (4) Jack Eugene Mathes's age was the same (29) in 1964 as in 1966? (5) Likewise, only Gretchen Goldschmidt's funeral record (Exhibit L) identifies her parents. Exhibits M, N, and O do not identify parents, and there is nothing confirming how the administrator knows that Clara Goldschmidt and Babette Goldschmidt died without issue. (6) Was Frieda Goldschmidt married as "Freda" to Adolph Stockley before marrying Ferdinand Matthew Spilmer? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/29/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Family trees usually also show the name of each and any person an ancestor married and the date they married, which is also usually supported by marriage certificates or some other proof of the wedding/marriage, to support parental lineage.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS S. CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The copy of the will attached to the second amended petition is incomplete. (2) "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3.] The attorney, not the proposed personal representative [CRC 7.103(c)], claims that the declaration clarifying the discrepancy in the dates the first codicil was signed was attached to the first amended petition, which pleading was superseded by this second amended petition. (3) Notice is defective if mailed before a pleading is filed or, in the case now with e-filing, when the pleading is actually e-filed and able to be viewed. Although the second amended petition was uploaded and stamped 2/9/23, because our clerks are short-staffed, it was not actually e-filed and scanned (available for viewing) until 2/10/23. Attorneys should make sure a document has actually been scanned before mailing notice. (4) Notice must include the instructions regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (5) Item 6 of the Judicial Council form Petition for Probate specifically instructs a petitioner to check the FIRST box that applies. If a decedent is survived by issue, the issue are the heirs; a decedent's parent is not an heir unless no issue survived. The petitioner's verified supplement to her second amended petition must be filed, submitting (1) a complete copy of the will and (2) a copy of the declaration clarifying the conflicting signing dates in the first codicil. A supplement should stand on its own and not "answer" issues raised by an examiner. (PETITIONER SHOULD ADDRESS JUST THESE TWO ISSUES IN HER SUPPLEMENT; the superfluous boxes checked in items 6 and 7 of the second amended petition will be ignored by the Court.) A courtesy copy of (1) the supplement and (2) the proof of service of the Notice of Second Amended Petition to Administer Estate, showing the 7/11/23 hearing date, mailed to the son and his attorney at least 15 days before 7/11/23, must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/29/23. (Please do NOT file another JC form DE-120 Notice of Hearing; that is NOT the appropriate form of notice for this initial petition.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
